mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration reduces consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation signals demand overheating that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint high percentiles of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth yield positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports short-term equity momentum when the economy operates below full capacity.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in housing starts reflect tightening credit conditions and reduced consumer confidence, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in >65% of periods following two consecutive monthly housing starts declines.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor demand and rising household income, generating positive short-term equity returns through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to produce a positive rank IC over 5-day forward returns across multiple 1-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates a negative real yield environment that triggers expectations of aggressive central bank intervention, depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative real yield periods yield positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly real GDP growth confirms broad economic expansion and corporate revenue resilience, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3.0 percent correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of observations.
